headline: PepsiCo's sales jump after it cuts prices
summary: PepsiCo's decision to cut prices and some new snacks boosted demand in the first quarter.  Revenue jumped 8.5% to $19.44 billion in the January-March period compared to the same period a year ago, the Purchase, New York, company said Thursday.  PepsiCo began cutting prices on value brands like Chester's and Santitas last spring to win back customers exasperated by years of price hikes.

content.title: PepsiCo's sales jump after it cuts prices

PepsiCo began cutting prices on value brands like Chester's and Santitas last spring to win back customers exasperated by years of price hikes.

headline: AMZN's Increasing Focus on Everyday Essentials: Can it Drive Growth?
summary: Amazon pivots to everyday essentials to drive repeat demand and grocery growth, but rising logistics costs and fierce competition could pressure margins.

headline: Walmart Deepens Commitment to Massachusetts, Fueling Long-Term Impact and Future Statewide Investments
summary: BOSTON, April 16, 2026--Walmart announced continued reinvestment across Massachusetts, with plans in 2026 to remodel 13 stores as part of ongoing efforts to modernize the in-store and digital shopping experience—upgrading layouts, technology, and services to offer faster, more convenient shopping and delivery in as little as an hour for most customers. These efforts reflect Walmart’s commitment to supporting economic growth and strengthening communities statewide.

headline: Walmart is Investing in Stores to Drive Speed, Convenience and Growth
summary: BENTONVILLE, Ark., April 16, 2026--This year, we are making a significant investment in our stores across the United States, reinforcing our commitment to the communities we serve. This investment is intended to create jobs, help strengthen local economies, and make shopping faster and more convenient for our customers.
